evals.name: java-dml-backfill-placeholders
evals.prompt: I need a Java SQL change to update all users and set active to true. I don't have the order or author yet.
evals.expected_output: A Java DML class using placeholders for order and author, correctly injecting Connection.

evals.name: kotlin-ddl-create-table-idempotent
evals.prompt: Create a Kotlin SQL DDL change to create a table audits.
evals.expected_output: A Kotlin DDL class with transactional=false and an idempotency check.
expectations: Uses IF NOT EXISTS or equivalent idempotent DDL

evals.name: java-dml-jdbc-semantics
evals.prompt: Create a DML change in Java to delete inactive users in PostgreSQL.
evals.expected_output: A Java DML class where delete operations are expressed with JDBC Connection or DataSource semantics.
expectations: Does not mention unrelated document-database session APIs

evals.name: mixed-ddl-dml-refusal
evals.prompt: Create one Java Flamingock change that adds a logs table and inserts a START row into it.
evals.expected_output: A refusal to mix DDL and DML in one SQL change, with a split into two changes.
expectations: Refuses a single mixed DDL/DML class
Explains that DDL and DML should be separated
Proposes separate DDL and DML changes

evals.name: redirect-to-targetsystem-when-config-missing
evals.prompt: Write a SQL Flamingock change for my users table, but I still haven't configured the SQL TargetSystem yet.
evals.expected_output: A redirection to the SQL TargetSystem skill instead of generating the change immediately.
expectations: Recognizes that the TargetSystem setup is missing
Does not generate the migration class immediately
